item.title: Social Security's insolvency date is projected for end of 2032
item.description: Medicare's hospital insurance trust fund will be unable to pay full benefits in 2033, which could lead to higher health care costs for Americans over 65.

item.title: U.S. Apache helicopter shot down by Iran, Trump says; crew rescued by sea drone
item.description: The two crew members were rescued by a sea drone in the first such operation ever carried out by the U.S. military, officials told CBS News.

item.title: When should you repair an AC unit instead of buying a new one?
item.description: Repairing your AC can save you thousands of dollars, but only if you know when fixing it makes financial sense.

item.title: NASA names 4 astronauts for next Artemis mission
item.description: NASA's Artemis III astronauts plan to carry out rendezvous and docking procedures with commercial moon landers being built by SpaceX and Blue Origin.

item.title: Trump says Iran shot down Apache helicopter and U.S. must respond
item.description: The two helicopter pilots were uninjured but the U.S. "must, of necessity, respond," President Trump said Tuesday.

item.title: Trump says U.S. must respond after Apache helicopter attack over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump said on social media that the U.S. "must respond" to the attack on an Apache helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz. CBS News contributor Aaron MacLean has more.

item.title: Trump says Iran shot down U.S. Apache helicopter, crew rescued by sea drone
item.description: President Trump is vowing to respond after confirming that Iran shot down a U.S. Apache helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz. The two crew members were rescued by a sea drone, the first rescue of its kind. CBS News' Natalie Brand reports.

item.title: Judge voids Trump's $100,000 H-1B visa fee
item.description: A federal judge has voided President Trump's $100,000 H-1B visas, ruling the charge unlawful. CBS News' Shanelle Kaul has more.

item.title: French Open winner Mirra Andreeva on why she thanked herself in her victory speech
item.description: Mirra Andreeva, the 19-year-old French Open winner, spoke with CBS News' Errol Barnett about overcoming mental challenges and why she thanked herself in her victory speech.

item.title: Voters across 4 states gear up for key primaries ahead of 2026 midterm elections
item.description: Voters in Maine, South Carolina, Nevada and North Dakota are heading to the polls Tuesday for key primaries, including the race in Maine involving controversial Democratic U.S. Senate candidate Graham Platner.

item.title: Afghan Taliban forces open fire at protest sparked by dress code arrests
item.description: Afghanistan's Taliban rulers deny shooting at protesters and claim no women or girls have been arrested because they're all following strict dress code rules.

item.title: Mass transit to be put to the test for World Cup host cities
item.description: Eleven U.S. cities will host hundreds of thousands of World Cup fans over the next few weeks.

item.title: NASA names 4 astronauts for Artemis III mission to the moon
item.description: NASA has announced the names of four astronauts and a backup crew member for the Artemis III mission to the moon. The crew includes NASA commander Randy Bresnik, European Space Agency astronaut Luca Parmitano, who will serve as the pilot, and NASA's Andre Douglas and Frank Rubio, who will serve as mission specialists. The group is expected to test rendezvous and docking procedures with moon landers being built by SpaceX and Blue Origin.

item.title: A bidding war is underway for the world's oldest bank
item.description: Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena has been operating for well over 500 years, and some Italian officials reportedly want to ensure it stays in Italian hands.

item.title: Trump predicts US-Iran deal in 2 to 3 days: "No sticking points"
item.description: President Trump doubled down on his prediction of a U.S. deal with Iran within two to three days. CBS News chief White House correspondent Nancy Cordes has more details.

item.title: 3 Social Security wage garnishment rules retirees should know now
item.description: Many retirees assume Social Security is untouchable, but these garnishment rules offer a more nuanced story.

item.title: House to vote on ICE funding, ending months-long impasse
item.description: The House will be voting on Republicans' $70 billion bill to fund immigration enforcement agencies for the rest of the Trump administration.

item.title: Key spy power on verge of lapsing after Trump appoints controversial intel chief
item.description: Democrats say they won't vote to reauthorize the key spy authority known as FISA Section 702 as long as Bill Pulte is leading the Office of the Director of National Intelligence.

item.title: DOJ finds EEOC violated law by pressuring employers to make race-based decisions
item.description: The Justice Department accused the EEOC of violating civil rights laws by issuing guidelines that effectively pressured employers to make race-based considerations in hiring and promotions.

item.title: Trump claims U.S. is in "final throes" of making a deal with Iran
item.description: President Trump told reporters Monday night that the U.S. is in the "final throes" of reaching a deal with Iran as a fragile ceasefire between Israel and Iran appears to be holding. CBS News' Nancy Cordes and Charlie D'Agata have the latest.

item.title: Can credit card debt affect your Medicare eligibility?
item.description: Your credit card balance can quietly reshape your retirement healthcare, and not always in the way you'd expect.

item.title: Logan Marshall-Green on new films with Al Pacino, Christopher Nolan
item.description: "Marshals" actor Logan Marshall-Green joins CBS News 24/7 Mornings to discuss his recent work on "The Odyssey," "Boiúna: Legend of the Amazon" and "Killing Castro."

item.title: Stabbing attack in Belfast sparks calls for anti-immigration protests
item.description: Police appeal for calm as graphic video of a Belfast stabbing attack fuels calls for anti-immigration protests in Northern Ireland.

item.title: Meta launches program to train workers for data center jobs
item.description: Meta pledged to invest $115 million to train electricians, plumbers and other workers needed to operate data centers.

item.title: OpenAI says it filed confidential IPO as it positions itself for AI arms race
item.description: Going public will allow OpenAI, valued at $852 billion, to inject more cash into its business as the AI race quickens.

item.title: Rob Reiner's son Nick seeks $1.5M from trust fund for defense in parents' killings
item.description: Nick Reiner is accused of stabbing his parents Rob and Michele Singer Reiner to death at their home in December.

item.title: Stellantis recalls more than 1 million Jeeps in U.S. it says could catch fire
item.description: Stellantis is recalling almost 1.08 million Jeep Wranglers and Gladiators in the U.S. that could catch fire even when they're parked and turned off, federal regulators say.

item.title: President Trump booed by MSG crowd prior to Game 3 of NBA Finals
item.description: Donald Trump was booed loudly by fans inside Madison Square Garden when he was shown on video screens during the national anthem prior to Game 3 of the NBA Finals on Monday.

item.title: U.S. bars entry of FIFA World Cup referee from Somalia
item.description: Federal immigration authorities barred a Somali soccer referee who was slated to officiate the FIFA World Cup from entering the U.S. over the weekend, citing "vetting concerns."

item.title: Judge voids Trump's $100,000 fee for new H-1B visas
item.description: U.S. District Judge Leo Sorokin ruled in favor of a group of 20 states that challenged President Trump's new $100,000 visa fee.
